metadata.archetype_justification: MySQL database with CRUD operations on users and shopping baskets (POST/DELETE basket items, POST user), entity lifecycle management with UUID-based identifiers, and user-specific data access patterns.
metadata.context: Legacy Java Spring Boot monolith on EC2 with MySQL — primary decomposition target. The agent needs access to order and return data through discrete service APIs.

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: OAuth2 configured with @EnableResourceServer but all endpoints set to permitAll(). No authentication enforced. No machine identity (service account, API key, mTLS, or client credentials flow) exists.
findings.gap: No mechanism to authenticate agent callers or attribute actions to specific agent identities.
findings.recommendation: Implement API Gateway with IAM/Cognito authorizers or OAuth2 client credentials flow with principal attribution.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Deploy API Gateway with IAM or Cognito authorizers in front of EC2 instance
blocker_remediation.target_state: Every API call authenticated with machine-readable principal identifier in audit logs

findings.title: No Scoped Permissions
findings.description: All endpoints use @PreAuthorize("permitAll()"). No role differentiation, no scoped permissions, no mechanism to grant read-only vs read-write access per caller.
findings.gap: Cannot create a scoped agent identity with restricted access.
findings.recommendation: Implement RBAC with read-only and read-write roles enforced at Spring Security or API Gateway level.

compensating_controls: API Gateway resource policies restricting agent identities to specific endpoints
Network-level segmentation

findings.title: No Action-Level Authorization
findings.description: No action-level authorization. All endpoints (read and write) open to any caller with identical access.
findings.gap: Cannot restrict an agent to read-only access while allowing another agent write access.
findings.recommendation: Implement method-level security annotations or API Gateway method-level auth.

compensating_controls: API Gateway method-level authorization
Network segmentation exposing only read endpoints

findings.title: No Identity Propagation
findings.description: No identity propagation. User context passed as URL parameter (userUuid), not from authenticated token. Cannot distinguish agent-as-self vs agent-on-behalf-of-user.
findings.gap: Cannot determine whether an API call was made by an agent under its own authority or on behalf of a specific user.
findings.recommendation: Implement JWT-based identity propagation with both agent and delegated user identity.

compensating_controls: Require agents to pass X-On-Behalf-Of header for logging
Restrict agent to operations not requiring user delegation

findings.title: Hardcoded Database Credentials
findings.description: Database credentials hardcoded in application.properties: username MonoToMicroUser and password MonoToMicroPassword. No secrets management system used.
findings.gap: Hardcoded credentials with no rotation. Prompt injection or config exposure leaks database access.
findings.recommendation: Migrate credentials to AWS Secrets Manager with IAM-based access and automatic rotation.

compensating_controls: Restrict database network access to EC2 security group only
Rotate credentials manually on schedule

findings.title: No Audit Logging
findings.description: No audit logging exists. No CloudTrail, no application-level audit log, no immutable log storage. Only System.out.println for EC2 metadata in HealthController.
findings.gap: Cannot determine who made any API call, when, or what the outcome was.
findings.recommendation: Implement structured audit logging with principal attribution stored in CloudWatch Logs or S3 with Object Lock.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents still require audit trails for compliance but immutable logging is not a deployment blocker under read-only scope.
compensating_controls: Deploy ALB/API Gateway with access logging
Enable CloudTrail for AWS API audit

findings.title: No Agent Identity Suspension Mechanism
findings.description: No identity management system exists. Authentication is disabled (permitAll()), so no mechanism to revoke or suspend any identity.
findings.gap: Cannot isolate or disable a misbehaving agent without taking down the entire application.
findings.recommendation: Implement API key or OAuth2 client identity with revocation mechanism.

compensating_controls: API Gateway API keys with revocation
Network-level blocking via security groups

findings.title: No Compensation or Rollback
findings.description: No compensation or rollback mechanisms. Basket operations are single INSERT/DELETE statements with no transaction coordination. No saga pattern, no undo endpoints.
findings.gap: Partial state cannot be reverted if multi-step workflows fail.
findings.recommendation: Implement compensation endpoints and wrap multi-step operations in database transactions.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write workflows, but compensation capability is relevant for system maturity and future write-enabled scope.
compensating_controls: Restrict agent to read-only operations
Add remove-from-basket as compensation for add-to-basket

findings.title: No Rate Limiting
findings.description: No rate limiting at any layer. No API Gateway, no WAF, no application-level middleware. Application accepts unlimited requests.
findings.gap: Runaway agent loops can overwhelm the application at machine speed.
findings.recommendation: Deploy API Gateway with throttling or add rate limiting middleware.

compensating_controls: Deploy ALB with connection limits
API Gateway with usage plans

findings.title: Sensitive Data Exposed via API Without Filtering
findings.description: User PII (email, first_name, last_name) stored in unicorn_user table and returned via API responses with no filtering. Login endpoint returns full User object including email.
findings.gap: B1: PII fields (email) exposed in API responses. B2: No access control differentiation. B3: No formal classification.
findings.recommendation: Create agent-facing response DTOs excluding PII. Implement OAuth scopes for data access differentiation.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, B1 fires as RISK-SAFETY
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Under read-only scope, B1 fires as RISK-SAFETY because PII fields are returned in general API responses. B2 fires as RISK-SAFETY due to no access differentiation. Overall DATA-Q1 = RISK-SAFETY.

b1.reasoning: User email returned in login response without @JsonIgnore. Under read-only scope, B1 resolves to RISK-SAFETY.

b2.reasoning: All callers get identical access via permitAll(). No differentiation between sensitive and non-sensitive data access.

b3.reasoning: No formal data classification metadata (tags, annotations, or catalog entries).

compensating_controls: Create separate response DTOs excluding email for agent endpoints
Field-level serialization control with @JsonView

findings.title: No Data Residency Controls
findings.description: No data residency configuration. User PII stored with no documented residency requirements or region constraints.
findings.gap: No mechanism to prevent agent from transmitting PII to LLM endpoint in different region.
findings.recommendation: Document data residency requirements. Configure agent orchestration for same-region LLM endpoints.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Data residency is relevant for read-only agents accessing PII but risk profile is lower without data modification.
compensating_controls: Document data classification and residency requirements
Configure agent orchestration to use same-region LLM endpoints

findings.title: No PII Redaction Controls in Logs
findings.description: No PII redaction controls exist. Current logging is minimal but no safeguards against PII leakage if log verbosity increases.
findings.gap: No systematic PII protection in logging pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Implement structured logging with PII field masking before enabling detailed logging.

compensating_controls: Keep logging at minimal level
Add log scrubbing before increasing verbosity

findings.title: No Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, Swagger, AsyncAPI, or machine-readable spec exists. API defined only in Java annotations.
findings.gap: Agent tool definitions must be manually authored. Drift between tools and implementation likely.
findings.recommendation: Add springdoc-openapi-ui dependency to auto-generate OpenAPI spec.

findings.title: No Structured Error Responses
findings.description: Error responses are bare HTTP status codes with empty bodies. ResponseEntity(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST) with no error detail.
findings.gap: Agent cannot distinguish retriable from terminal errors or identify failed fields.
findings.recommendation: Implement @ControllerAdvice with structured JSON error bodies.

findings.title: No Graceful Degradation Signaling
findings.description: Health endpoints provide binary status only. No machine-readable degradation indicators or freshness headers.
findings.gap: Agents cannot detect degraded mode operation.
findings.recommendation: Enhance health endpoints with granular JSON status. Add X-Data-Freshness headers.

findings.title: No Sandbox/Staging Environment
findings.description: No sandbox, staging, or local development environment configuration. No Docker Compose, no environment profiles.
findings.gap: No safe environment to test agent behavior.
findings.recommendation: Create Docker Compose setup with MySQL for local/staging agent testing.

findings.title: No Pagination or Filtering
findings.description: GET /unicorns returns ALL records. Unbounded SELECT queries with no LIMIT, pagination, or filtering.
findings.gap: Agents receive entire datasets. Context window exhaustion risk.
findings.recommendation: Add pagination parameters to list endpoints.

findings.title: Minimal Input Validation
findings.description: Only null checks in controllers. No validation framework, no @Valid annotations, no structured validation errors. SQL uses parameterized queries preventing injection.
findings.gap: Malformed agent payloads not rejected with useful feedback.
findings.recommendation: Add spring-boot-starter-validation with Bean Validation annotations.

findings.title: Temporal Metadata Not Exposed
findings.description: Database has creation_date and last_modified_date columns but API does not expose them. No freshness headers.
findings.gap: Agents cannot determine data freshness from API responses.
findings.recommendation: Map timestamp fields to response DTOs. Add Last-Modified headers.

findings.title: No Schema Versioning or API Contracts
findings.description: No API versioning, no schema versioning, no breaking change detection, no CI/CD pipeline.
findings.gap: Agent tool bindings break silently on API changes.
findings.recommendation: Version API paths and implement contract testing in CI.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing or Structured Logging
findings.description: No X-Ray, no OpenTelemetry, no structured logging. Only System.out.println. No correlation IDs.
findings.gap: Cannot trace or debug agent-initiated requests.
findings.recommendation: Add X-Ray SDK or OpenTelemetry. Implement structured JSON logging.

findings.title: No Alerting Configuration
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms, no anomaly detection, no PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration.
findings.gap: Cannot detect target system degradation proactively.
findings.recommendation: Deploy CloudWatch alarms for error rates and latency.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC exists. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, or Helm. Infrastructure presumably managed manually.
findings.gap: Agent-facing infrastructure is unauditable and non-reproducible.
findings.recommendation: Define infrastructure in CloudFormation or CDK.

findings.title: No CI/CD Pipeline
findings.description: No CI/CD configuration. No GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, or CodeBuild.
findings.gap: API changes deployed without automated verification.
findings.recommendation: Implement CI/CD with contract testing.

findings.title: No Rollback Capability
findings.description: No deployment or rollback configuration. Manual JAR deployment to EC2.
findings.gap: Cannot roll back broken deployments affecting agent APIs.
findings.recommendation: Implement CodeDeploy with health-check-based rollback.

findings.title: Zero Test Coverage
findings.description: No test files exist. Test dependency in build.gradle unused. No unit, integration, or API tests.
findings.gap: No automated verification of API behavior.
findings.recommendation: Create integration tests for controller endpoints.

findings.title: Encryption at Rest Unverifiable
findings.description: No IaC to verify KMS encryption on database storage. No KMS references.
findings.gap: PII may be unencrypted at rest.
findings.recommendation: Enable RDS/EBS encryption with KMS. Document in IaC.

findings.title: Partial Idempotency via Database Constraints
findings.description: INSERT IGNORE and UNIQUE constraints provide database-level idempotency for basket operations. No explicit idempotency key support.
findings.gap: No request-level idempotency mechanism for general write operations.
findings.recommendation: Implement idempotency keys when expanding to write-enabled scope.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations, so idempotency is informational only.

findings.title: JSON Response Format
findings.description: All API responses use JSON via Jackson. Agent-friendly format requiring no additional parsing.
findings.gap: None.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.title: No Event Emission
findings.description: No event emission. Internal event objects are in-process DTOs, not distributed events. No webhooks, SNS, SQS, or EventBridge.
findings.gap: Agents cannot subscribe to state changes reactively.
findings.recommendation: Add EventBridge integration during decomposition.

findings.title: No Rate Limit Headers
findings.description: No rate limit documentation or X-RateLimit-Remaining/Retry-After headers.
findings.gap: Agents have no visibility into call budget.
findings.recommendation: Add rate limit headers when implementing STATE-Q5 rate limiting.

findings.title: No Concurrency Controls
findings.description: No optimistic locking, no version fields, no ETags. INSERT IGNORE prevents duplicates but not general concurrency control.
findings.gap: Concurrent writes could cause race conditions under write-enabled scope.
findings.recommendation: Add version fields and optimistic locking when expanding scope.

findings.title: No Transaction Limits
findings.description: No transaction limits or blast radius controls. No configurable limits on operations per session.
findings.gap: Under write-enabled scope, unbounded operations possible.
findings.recommendation: Implement per-caller transaction limits for write-enabled scope.

findings.title: No Draft/Pending State
findings.description: No draft or pending state. All writes commit immediately.
findings.gap: No human review opportunity for agent-proposed changes.
findings.recommendation: Add PENDING status for write operations under write-enabled scope.

findings.title: No Approval Gates
findings.description: No approval gate mechanism. All operations execute immediately.
findings.gap: Cannot configure human approval for specific operations.
findings.recommendation: Consider Step Functions approval tasks for high-risk operations.

findings.title: No Data Quality Metrics
findings.description: No data quality monitoring, profiling, or completeness metrics.
findings.gap: Cannot assess data reliability for agent decisions.
findings.recommendation: Add data quality monitoring.

findings.title: No Data Catalog
findings.description: No data catalog or metadata layer. Only schema reference is create_tables.sql.
findings.gap: Agent tool builders must reverse-engineer the data model.
findings.recommendation: Document data model in structured format.

findings.title: No Business Outcome Metrics
findings.description: No custom business metrics. Only infrastructure metrics via Spring Boot Actuator.
findings.gap: Cannot measure agent interaction quality.
findings.recommendation: Publish business metrics (basket operations, registrations) to CloudWatch.

evaluations.title: Documented API Interface
evaluations.reason: Application exposes REST endpoints via Spring Boot @RestController annotations. Integration does not require direct database access or UI automation.

evaluations.title: Asynchronous Operation Support
evaluations.reason: Extended question not triggered — no operations exceeding 30s or long-running workflows detected in this simple CRUD application.

evaluations.title: Queryable Current State
evaluations.reason: Application exposes GET /unicorns and GET /unicorns/basket/{userUuid} for querying current state.

evaluations.title: Circuit Breakers and Resilience
evaluations.reason: Extended question not triggered — service does not call other business services or external APIs. EC2 metadata is infrastructure-level, not a business dependency.

evaluations.title: Semantically Meaningful Field Names
evaluations.reason: Field names are human-readable and semantically meaningful: uuid, email, firstName, lastName, name, description, price, image. No legacy abbreviations requiring data dictionaries.

items.summary: Resolve the authentication blocker and establish foundational safety controls: machine identity, scoped permissions, audit logging, rate limiting, and sensitive data protection.
Build operational maturity: API documentation, structured errors, observability, CI/CD, IaC, and data quality improvements.
Prepare for write-enabled scope and event-driven patterns: idempotency, concurrency controls, approval workflows, event emission, and test coverage.

recommended_actions.action: Deploy API Gateway with Cognito/IAM authorizers
recommended_actions.rationale: Resolves the only BLOCKER (AUTH-Q1) and simultaneously addresses scoped permissions, action-level auth, identity suspension, and rate limiting via a single API Gateway deployment.

recommended_actions.action: Migrate credentials to AWS Secrets Manager
recommended_actions.rationale: Quick win that eliminates hardcoded credential risk. Low effort, high security impact.
Required for compliance and forensics. Enable CloudTrail and application-level audit interceptor.
Auto-generates OpenAPI spec from existing annotations. Enables automated agent tool generation.

recommended_actions.action: Implement CI/CD pipeline with contract testing
recommended_actions.rationale: Foundation for all deployment safety: automated testing, rollback, and breaking change detection.

recommended_actions.action: Create agent-facing response DTOs with PII filtering
recommended_actions.rationale: Prevents PII exposure to agent-facing endpoints without requiring full auth overhaul.
